title: pleomele goldieana
intro: Pleomele goldieana, also known as the Hawaiian Ti plant or Kohina, is a species of flowering plant in the Asparagaceae family, native to the Pacific Islands, particularly Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pleomele goldieana include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, and it is also used in traditional Hawaiian medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
